question: Calculate the break-even budget for an average film.
doc_context: Industry standard assumes 2x production budget as minimum break-even when including P&A costs. The average film in 2025 needed $34M in production plus $9.2M in marketing, requiring $86.4M in revenue to break even accounting for exhibitor splits (typically 45-50% of box office).
answer: Average film requires $34M production + $9.2M marketing = $43.2M total cost. At 50% exhibitor split, break-even requires ~$86M box office. The average film at $52M revenue does not break even theatrically, relying on ancillary markets.
